body.title: [awaiting_validation] Remove the dead ADC-named launcher wrappers (bin/adc, bin/adc.ps1)
body.intention: No 'adc' (Agent Data Center) launcher artifact survives anywhere in the repo; the name is a dead migration stage (Igor -> Utility Closet -> Agent Data Center -> Unseen University).
body.description:
ADC (Agent Data Center) is a retired migration stage of this project; the `adc` launcher name is wrong wherever it appears. bin/adc and bin/adc.ps1 are the only operational adc artifacts — POSIX + Windows bootstrap wrappers that create a venv then delegate to the still-live `agentctl` CLI. uu (bin/uu) is the current view/control dispatcher. Remove both adc wrappers. agentctl itself stays (live console_scripts entry in pyproject.toml, documented installer CLI in README) — it is not 'adc'. Historical memory records (T-adc-*), the retired adc.palace Postgres schema, and CLAUDE.md phase-decision references are legitimate history and out of scope.

**Affected files:** bin/adc (delete), bin/adc.ps1 (delete)
**Design rules:** none apply (deleting dead launcher scripts; no memory-shape, no persistence, no sqlite). delete-after-successful-merge pattern: uu already replaced adc, so this is the remove-behind step.
**Scope boundary:** IN — delete bin/adc + bin/adc.ps1. OUT — agentctl (live), adc.palace schema, historical T-adc-* memory records, CLAUDE.md phase refs, and the separate stale root superclaude/superclaude.ps1 duplicates (different issue, flagged separately).
**Test plan:** After deletion: `git ls-files | grep -E '(^|/)adc(\.|$)'` returns empty; `which adc` no longer resolves into the repo; `pip install -e .` still succeeds (adc was not a packaged entry point — only agentctl is). No automated test for launcher-script presence exists.
**Completion criteria:** bin/adc and bin/adc.ps1 are gone from the tree, the grep above is empty, and the removal is committed referencing this ticket.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.result: Removed bin/adc + bin/adc.ps1 (dead ADC bootstrap wrappers). Proof: git ls-files grep for adc launcher files is empty; which adc no longer resolves into repo. agentctl left intact (live console_scripts entry). pip install unaffected (adc was not a packaged entry point).
